issues.title: Knowledge curator agent (phase-1 deploy: KB ingest + progress + AGENTS.md browse)
issues.body:
Phase-1 deployment's centerpiece: turn openearth into a system centered on a **Knowledge curator agent**, deployed to claude / opencode / codex.

## Scope (locked)
- **3 agents**: claude, opencode, codex.
- **KB ingest**: agent-triggered (`kb-ingest` skill → `openearth knowledge ingest`), async, file-level queue progress.
- **KB use**: AGENTS.md pointer (installed at plugin-install time, NOT by the curator) → coding agent self-browses `Knowledge/`.
- **Curator daemon** (the Knowledge agent, same backend as reflect): full-takeover per-file ingest (extract→compile→place), organize, lint, periodic reflect → pending-question queue (anti-spam via Knowledge/ lineage).
- **Out**: trajectory→skill (phase-2), CS, Tool domain (all deferred).

## Architecture
Six domains → six logical agents (same tool-using backend; reflect = Skills agent ✅ built, **curator = Knowledge agent** 🔴 this issue). External agents **only call `openearth` CLI** — never touch internal agents/store directly. Progress = a queue **file** under `Knowledge/.queue/` that the curator updates per file and `openearth knowledge progress` reads directly (no agent routing).

## MVP build order
1. Queue file + `ingest` (non-blocking enqueue) + `progress` (read file) + stub daemon → close the loop "add folder → query progress".
2. Curator daemon per-file full processing (reuse `dataconnector` / `KnowledgeCompiler`, migrate to skill-driven).
3. `kb-ingest` skill + install-time pointer.
4. Curator-ledger (domain lineage) + periodic reflect + pending-question queue.
5. (later) Tool domain, Trajectory/Benchmark/Memory agents; extract the "domain-agent" framework from reflect+curator.

## Full design
See [docs/knowledge-curator-design.md](https://github.com/coo-2022/OpenEarth/blob/docs/knowledge-curator-design/docs/knowledge-curator-design.md) (on the `docs/knowledge-curator-design` branch).

## Open follow-ups (separate issues, later)
- Tool domain (6th domain + tool agent).
- Trajectory / Benchmark / Memory agents.
- Extract the domain-agent framework (after curator lands).
- Proactive push channel (near-term = pending-question queue surfaced next interaction).

🤖 Filed from a design session.

pulls.title: feat(memory): AGFS memory plane + unified evolution lifecycle (default-frozen, opt-in)
pulls.body:
## What
Vendors the AGFS memory runtime closure (39-file clean subset + adapter) into
`third_party/agfs_memory/`, wires it as a pluggable memory plane, and adds a
unified evolution lifecycle skeleton (`openearth/services/evolution/`: registry +
mutex + harm-UCB gate + composite-version ledger, modes frozen/shadow/gated).

## Safety — default behavior is bit-identical
- `run.memory_backend` defaults to `none` → memory plane unchanged.
- `evolution.mode` defaults to `frozen` → the evolution loop runs zero lines.
- Self-contained: no AGFS checkout / pip needed (vendored copy prepended to sys.path).
- `crud=shadow` records would-delete only (dry_run), never deletes.
Every existing test + `openearth run`/`experience-server` behaves identically
unless config is changed. See `docs/agfs-unified-evolution-handoff.md`.

## Merge shape
23 commits ahead of main, no conflicts (main is an ancestor). 82 files, mostly
additive. Suggest squash-merge if the team prefers a compact history.

## Not included (deliberate, follow-up)
Live candidate proposer + gate wired to RolloutResult (the G2 step) — the skeleton
is observable in `shadow` mode but does not self-evolve production policy yet.
